reasoning: Bobby Finke's long course 1500m freestyle world record of 14:30.67, set at the Paris 2024 Olympics, has comfortably survived the peak long course events of 2025. At the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore (July-August), the event was won by Ahmed Jaouadi in 14:34.41—well off Finke's record. Furthermore, the global swimming calendar from October through February is heavily oriented towards short course (25m) competitions, such as the World Aquatics Swimming World Cup and Short Course World Championships. Opportunities for athletes to race fully tapered in long course meters during the winter are exceptionally rare. Given the historical longevity of the 1500m freestyle world record (only 4 record holders in the last 30+ years) and the lack of premier long course competitions between November 2025 and February 2026, the chances of the record being broken are minimal. The slight decrease in probability over time reflects the marginally compounding risk of a rare winter long course effort by an emerging competitor (like Jaouadi or Wiffen) or highly remote possibilities such as retrospective doping disqualifications.
